title: astragalus monspessulanus subsp. gypsophilus
intro: Astragalus monspessulanus subsp. gypsophilus, also known as Gypsophila astragalus, is a plant species in the legume family that belongs to the Astragalus genus. It is a perennial herb with small, yellow or white flowers, native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Astragalus monspessulanus subsp. gypsophilus lie in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including immune system support, anti-aging properties, and cardiovascular protection, and is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal purposes.
